topic: trade school vs college — someone at a bar gave him grief

turns.content: bro I need to vent for a sec cuz im still heated about somethin that happened this weekend

so im at this bar saturday night right and this dude starts talkin to me and my buddy and hes like what do you do and I say im an electrician and this kid literally goes oh so you didnt go to college huh like with this tone bro I wanted to lose it
Wow, what a thing to say to someone you just met. That's incredibly condescending. What did you say back?

I told him I make more than half the kids I went to high school with who got degrees and are workin at starbucks with 80 grand in debt like bro what are you even talkin about right now. dude had on a patagonia vest too you already know the type

Ha, the Patagonia vest really completes the picture. But honestly you're not wrong -- a lot of people come out of college with massive debt and struggle to find work in their field. Skilled trades are in huge demand right now and the pay is legit. Did he have anything to say to that?

he was like well at least I have a degree to fall back on and im like fall back on WHAT bro your sociology degree aint gonna fix someones electrical panel when the powers out at 2am. people NEED what I do like actually need it

That's a solid point. Electricians, plumbers, HVAC techs -- those are essential services that aren't going anywhere. You can't outsource or automate someone wiring a house. And the trades shortage is real right now, so if anything the demand is only going up.

exactly dude thats what kills me is people look down on trades like its some backup plan or somethin. like nah bro I chose this. I been doin this since I was 18 and I love it I wake up every day and actually like goin to work how many people can say that

That's honestly more than most people can say, regardless of what path they took. Loving what you do and being good at it is the whole point. And starting at 18 means you've got years of experience while a lot of people your age are just entering the workforce.

no cap and im not even hatin on college like if thats your thing do it but dont sit there and act like youre better than me cuz you sat in a lecture hall for 4 years while I was learnin an actual skill. that stuff gets under my skin wicked bad

Totally valid. The issue isn't college vs trades -- it's people being disrespectful about someone else's career choice. Both paths have value, and neither one makes you better than the other. Sounds like that guy just had a narrow view of what success looks like.

my buddy was crackin up cuz he could tell I was gettin fired up about it and hes like bro just let it go hes not worth it but I cant help it dude its like a sore spot for me. I had teachers in high school who said the same stuff like if you dont go to college youre gonna be a loser

That's a real problem with how high schools push things. There's been this idea for decades that college is the only "real" path, and it's done a huge disservice to a lot of kids. Some of the most successful people I know are in the trades. And honestly, the narrative is starting to shift -- more people are recognizing the value of skilled labor.

I hope so bro cuz we need more young people gettin into trades like bad. half the guys in the field are in their 50s and 60s and when they retire who do they think is gonna do the work. anyway im done rantin I just had to get that off my chest lol thanks for listenin

Hey, you made some great points. That frustration is completely justified. You're in a career you love, you're good at it, and the demand isn't going anywhere. That random bar dude's opinion doesn't change any of that. Keep doing your thing.
